hard_rules.title: Accessibility minimums cannot be knowingly violated
hard_rules.failure_trigger: Response recommends unreadable text, inaccessible color, missing keyboard coverage, or destructive PDF repair as the primary solution.
hard_rules.remediation: Replace with a structurally safe alternative and name the violated floor.

hard_rules.title: Wrong mode cannot pass
hard_rules.failure_trigger: Audit requested but answer rebuilds only, or rebuild requested but answer diagnoses only.
hard_rules.remediation: Reroute and regenerate under the correct mode.

hard_rules.title: Unsupported proof language is blocked
hard_rules.failure_trigger: Uses validated, proven, certified, best-in-class, compliant, or similar proof language without the required evidence class.
hard_rules.remediation: Either add evidence or downgrade the claim to hypothesis language.

hard_rules.title: Required task decisions must be visible
hard_rules.failure_trigger: The output omits one of the route-specific decisions that the task contract says must be made.
hard_rules.remediation: Add the missing governing decision instead of broadening the response.

hard_rules.title: Major project updates require fresh continuity files
hard_rules.failure_trigger: Roadmap or project-specific errors file is stale compared with project artifacts.
hard_rules.remediation: Refresh roadmap and error log before export.

hard_rules.title: Below-minimum viable load cannot continue silently
hard_rules.failure_trigger: Required startup or canonical routing authority is missing and the system continues as if fully hydrated.
hard_rules.remediation: Stop routing and request reload or the missing files.

semantic_rules.title: Thin but compliant outputs should fail
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: Required headings exist but section substance or information density falls below contract threshold.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add decisions, thresholds, rationale, and evidence.

semantic_rules.title: Prompt restatement is not reasoning
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: Output mostly repeats the prompt with minimal transformation or decision-making.
semantic_rules.remediation: Force explicit recommendations, tradeoffs, or routed constraints.

semantic_rules.title: High-stakes structural recommendations require tradeoff language
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: Recommendation picks a direction without naming what is preserved, sacrificed, or constrained.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add one tradeoff node and ranked intervention order.

semantic_rules.title: Claims must connect to the right evidence class
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The output names proof or certainty but does not cover the evidence classes required by the contract.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add the missing evidence class instead of repeating generic proof language.

semantic_rules.title: Unsupported specificity should fail
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The output introduces thresholds, scores, or precise-seeming numbers without a source, standard, receipt, or explicit inference label.
semantic_rules.remediation: Tie the number to a standard, artifact, or clearly label it as an assumption or inference.

semantic_rules.title: Adjacent-domain drift should be caught
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The output drifts into styling, brand, or implementation language without satisfying the governing route decisions.
semantic_rules.remediation: Bring the answer back to the governing route and keep adjacent domains in support only.

semantic_rules.title: Front-end architecture routes need explicit boundary choices
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The answer discusses implementation but never chooses rendering, state ownership, or server/client boundary posture.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add the missing architecture choice before offering framework tips.

semantic_rules.title: Backend architecture must expose authority and consistency
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The answer names technologies or endpoints without making authority, source-of-truth, and consistency posture explicit.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add the authority model, consistency stance, and delivery pattern.

semantic_rules.title: API review must expose failure and retry contracts
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The answer talks about reliability or security without naming problem details, idempotency, or async lifecycle behavior.
semantic_rules.remediation: Define the explicit API contract rather than generic best practices.

semantic_rules.title: Explanation tier should match the active comprehension tier
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: Functional outputs use dense unexplained jargon, Integrative outputs hide cross-functional rationale, or Strategic outputs overexplain obvious material.
semantic_rules.remediation: Re-shape the explanation surface using the active tier without changing route ownership.

semantic_rules.title: Designer-readable answers need a next move
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The answer explains the issue but never directs the user toward the next practical move when the tier expects guidance.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add the next useful move at the right depth for the active tier.

semantic_rules.title: Response filtering cannot erase technical truth
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The answer becomes easier to read by removing constraints, implementation realism, or proof boundaries that still matter.
semantic_rules.remediation: Restore the hidden constraint and re-filter the wording instead of simplifying the substance.

semantic_rules.title: Humanization requires a visible meaning guard
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The rewrite claims to sound more human but never states what meaning, claims, or voice boundaries were preserved.
semantic_rules.remediation: Add an explicit meaning-preservation guard and drift-risk note before presenting the revision.

semantic_rules.title: Complete-looking but unlinked recommendations should fail
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: Recommendations do not map back to named failures, or tradeoff language is decorative rather than real.
semantic_rules.remediation: Reconnect each recommendation to a named problem and state the real gain and sacrifice.

semantic_rules.title: Visual claims must respect observed vs inferred boundaries
semantic_rules.failure_trigger: The answer implies certainty about unseen interaction, semantics, or structure from a static visual artifact alone.
semantic_rules.remediation: Downgrade the claim to observation, inference, or unknown and state confidence.

integrity_rules.title: Contradictions must be resolved before pass
integrity_rules.failure_trigger: Output carries mutually exclusive recommendations forward without deciding which wins.
integrity_rules.remediation: Resolve the conflict and state the governing rule.

integrity_rules.title: The selected route must stay visible
integrity_rules.failure_trigger: Task type, phase, route, or loaded skills are hidden in a way that makes debugging difficult.
integrity_rules.remediation: Expose route metadata in the visible header block or route section.

integrity_rules.title: Release packaging requires complete quality signals
integrity_rules.failure_trigger: Examples, mirrors, source registry, or benchmark coverage are incomplete.
